content: Hi everyone, I'm just starting to learn Julia (coming from the Python deep learning world). I was wondering if there's an equivalent of ?? (for showing current function's source code) when working with Julia in Jupyter Notebooks. I tried @code_string from the "CodeTracking" package but it doesn't always work. I also tried the @lessmacro but it always shows me the entire module instead of just the current function.

There's been an open issue about this for several yaers, the CodeTracking package seems to be the current recommendation there. Does it not reliably work even with Revise.jl loaded too? I haven't used it since I don't use a notebook much.
There's also the @edit macro, in case you weren't aware - which also loads the entire file, but in an external editor, so it might be easier to navigate.
